python -c "
import pyodbc

conn = pyodbc.connect(
    'DRIVER={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};'
    'SERVER=ushou102-exap1;DATABASE=searates;UID=sean;PWD=4peiling;'
)
cursor = conn.cursor()

query = \"\"\"
SELECT 
    h.TrackNumber,
    h.Sealine_Code,
    h.Sealine_Name,
    h.Status,
    v.Name AS Vessel_Name,
    v.imo,
    v.flag,
    l.Name AS Location_Name,
    l.Country,
    l.Country_Code,
    CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) AS Lat,
    CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) AS Lng
FROM sealine_header h
JOIN sealine_vessels v ON h.TrackNumber = v.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations l ON h.TrackNumber = l.TrackNumber
WHERE h.Status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
  AND l.Lat IS NOT NULL AND l.Lng IS NOT NULL
  AND TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
  AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
  AND (
      -- Red Sea corridor (Houthi threat zone)
      (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45)
      OR
      -- Gulf of Aden
      (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52)
      OR
      -- Persian Gulf / Gulf of Oman
      (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60)
      OR
      -- Eastern Mediterranean (Israel/Gaza/Lebanon/Syria)
      (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37)
  )
ORDER BY h.TrackNumber, l.Name
\"\"\"

cursor.execute(query)
cols = [col[0] for col in cursor.description]
rows = cursor.fetchall()

print(f'Total records found: {len(rows)}')
print()
print(' | '.join(cols))
print('-' * 120)
for row in rows:
    print(' | '.join(str(v) if v is not None else 'N/A' for v in row))

cursor.close()
conn.close()
"

input.command:
python -c "
import pyodbc

conn = pyodbc.connect(
    'DRIVER={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};'
    'SERVER=ushou102-exap1;DATABASE=searates;UID=sean;PWD=4peiling;'
)
cursor = conn.cursor()

query = \"\"\"
SELECT DISTINCT
    h.TrackNumber,
    h.Sealine_Code,
    h.Sealine_Name,
    v.Name AS Vessel_Name,
    v.imo,
    v.flag,
    l.Name AS Location_Name,
    l.Country,
    l.Country_Code,
    ROUND(TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT), 4) AS Lat,
    ROUND(TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT), 4) AS Lng,
    CASE
        WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45 THEN 'Red Sea'
        WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52 THEN 'Gulf of Aden'
        WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60 THEN 'Persian Gulf'
        WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37 THEN 'Eastern Mediterranean'
    END AS War_Zone
FROM sealine_header h
JOIN sealine_vessels v ON h.TrackNumber = v.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations l ON h.TrackNumber = l.TrackNumber
WHERE h.Status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
  AND TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
  AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
  AND (
      (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45)
      OR (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52)
      OR (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60)
      OR (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37)
  )
ORDER BY War_Zone, h.Sealine_Name, v.Name
\"\"\"

cursor.execute(query)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
cols = [col[0] for col in cursor.description]

# Group by war zone
from collections import defaultdict
zones = defaultdict(list)
for row in rows:
    zones[row[-1]].append(row)

for zone, zrows in sorted(zones.items()):
    print(f'\\n=== {zone} ({len(zrows)} records) ===')
    print(f'{\"TrackNumber\":<12} {\"Vessel_Name\":<30} {\"IMO\":<10} {\"Flag\":<5} {\"Location\":<25} {\"Country\":<20}')
    print('-' * 110)
    for r in zrows:
        print(f'{str(r[0]):<12} {str(r[3]):<30} {str(r[4]):<10} {str(r[5]):<5} {str(r[6]):<25} {str(r[7]):<20}')

print(f'\\nTotal distinct records: {len(rows)}')

cursor.close()
conn.close()
"

content.text: Still large. Let me get a clean summary with unique vessels per zone and export to Excel.

input.command:
python -c "
import pyodbc

conn = pyodbc.connect(
    'DRIVER={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};'
    'SERVER=ushou102-exap1;DATABASE=searates;UID=sean;PWD=4peiling;'
)
cursor = conn.cursor()

query = \"\"\"
SELECT
    War_Zone,
    COUNT(DISTINCT TrackNumber) AS Shipments,
    COUNT(DISTINCT Vessel_Name) AS Unique_Vessels,
    STRING_AGG(DISTINCT Country, ', ') AS Countries
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT
        h.TrackNumber,
        v.Name AS Vessel_Name,
        l.Country,
        CASE
            WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45 THEN 'Red Sea'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52 THEN 'Gulf of Aden'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60 THEN 'Persian Gulf'
            WHEN TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37 THEN 'Eastern Mediterranean'
        END AS War_Zone
    FROM sealine_header h
    JOIN sealine_vessels v ON h.TrackNumber = v.TrackNumber
    JOIN sealine_locations l ON h.TrackNumber = l.TrackNumber
    WHERE h.Status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
      AND TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
      AND (
          (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45)
          OR (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52)
          OR (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60)
          OR (TRY_CAST(l.Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(l.Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37)
      )
) sub
GROUP BY War_Zone
ORDER BY Shipments DESC
\"\"\"

cursor.execute(query)
print(f'{'War_Zone':<25} {'Shipments':>10} {'Unique_Vessels':>15}  Countries')
print('-' * 100)
for row in cursor.fetchall():
    print(f'{str(row[0]):<25} {row[1]:>10} {row[2]:>15}  {str(row[3])[:60]}')

cursor.close()
conn.close()
"

python -c "
import pyodbc

conn = pyodbc.connect(
    'DRIVER={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};'
    'SERVER=ushou102-exap1;DATABASE=searates;UID=sean;PWD=4peiling;'
)
cursor = conn.cursor()

query = \"\"\"
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT h.TrackNumber) AS Shipment_Count
FROM sealine_header h
JOIN (
    SELECT TrackNumber, MIN(Id) AS First_Id
    FROM sealine_locations
    GROUP BY TrackNumber
) first_loc ON h.TrackNumber = first_loc.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations l ON l.TrackNumber = first_loc.TrackNumber AND l.Id = first_loc.First_Id
WHERE l.LOCode LIKE 'USHOU%'
\"\"\"

cursor.execute(query)
print(f'Shipments starting from Houston (USHOU): {cursor.fetchone()[0]}')

# Also show breakdown by LOCode
query2 = \"\"\"
SELECT l.LOCode, l.Name, COUNT(DISTINCT h.TrackNumber) AS Shipments
FROM sealine_header h
JOIN (
    SELECT TrackNumber, MIN(Id) AS First_Id
    FROM sealine_locations
    GROUP BY TrackNumber
) first_loc ON h.TrackNumber = first_loc.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations l ON l.TrackNumber = first_loc.TrackNumber AND l.Id = first_loc.First_Id
WHERE l.LOCode LIKE 'USHOU%'
GROUP BY l.LOCode, l.Name
ORDER BY Shipments DESC
\"\"\"

cursor.execute(query2)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
print()
print(f'{\"LOCode\":<10} {\"Location\":<25} {\"Shipments\":>10}')
print('-' * 50)

The user wants to see a map showing all destinations from Houston port (USHOU) with IN_TRANSIT status, with the number of shipments per destination. 

I need to:
1. Query the database to get all shipments starting from Houston (USHOU, Id=1) with IN_TRANSIT status
2. Get the final destination (max Id) for each shipment
3. Group by destination and count shipments
4. Create a map visualization with bubble/circle markers sized by shipment count

Let me query the data first.

input.command:
python -c "
import pyodbc, json

conn = pyodbc.connect(
    'DRIVER={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};'
    'SERVER=ushou102-exap1;DATABASE=searates;UID=sean;PWD=4peiling;'
)
cursor = conn.cursor()

query = \"\"\"
SELECT
    dest.Name        AS Destination,
    dest.Country,
    dest.Country_Code,
    dest.LOCode,
    ROUND(TRY_CAST(dest.Lat AS FLOAT), 4) AS Lat,
    ROUND(TRY_CAST(dest.Lng AS FLOAT), 4) AS Lng,
    COUNT(DISTINCT h.TrackNumber)          AS Shipments
FROM sealine_header h
-- Origin: first stop is Houston
JOIN (
    SELECT TrackNumber, MIN(Id) AS First_Id
    FROM sealine_locations
    GROUP BY TrackNumber
) orig_seq ON h.TrackNumber = orig_seq.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations orig
    ON orig.TrackNumber = orig_seq.TrackNumber
   AND orig.Id = orig_seq.First_Id
   AND orig.LOCode LIKE 'USHOU%'
-- Destination: last stop
JOIN (
    SELECT TrackNumber, MAX(Id) AS Last_Id
    FROM sealine_locations
    GROUP BY TrackNumber
) dest_seq ON h.TrackNumber = dest_seq.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations dest
    ON dest.TrackNumber = dest_seq.TrackNumber
   AND dest.Id = dest_seq.Last_Id
   AND TRY_CAST(dest.Lat AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
   AND TRY_CAST(dest.Lng AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
WHERE h.Status = 'IN_TRANSIT'
GROUP BY dest.Name, dest.Country, dest.Country_Code, dest.LOCode, dest.Lat, dest.Lng
ORDER BY Shipments DESC
\"\"\"

cursor.execute(query)
rows = cursor.fetchall()
results = [{'name': r[0], 'country': r[1], 'country_code': r[2],
            'locode': r[3], 'lat': r[4], 'lng': r[5], 'shipments': r[6]} for r in rows]

print(f'Total destination groups: {len(results)}')
print(f'Total shipments: {sum(r[\"shipments\"] for r in results)}')
print(json.dumps(results[:5], indent=2))
cursor.close()
conn.close()
"

content.thinking: The user wants to see the SQL query used to generate the Houston destinations map data.

content.text:
```sql
SELECT
    dest.Name,
    dest.Country,
    dest.Country_Code,
    dest.LOCode,
    ROUND(TRY_CAST(dest.Lat AS FLOAT), 4) AS Lat,
    ROUND(TRY_CAST(dest.Lng AS FLOAT), 4) AS Lng,
    COUNT(DISTINCT h.TrackNumber) AS Shipments
FROM sealine_header h

-- Origin: first stop must be Houston
JOIN (
    SELECT TrackNumber, MIN(Id) AS First_Id
    FROM sealine_locations
    GROUP BY TrackNumber
) orig_seq ON h.TrackNumber = orig_seq.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations orig
    ON  orig.TrackNumber = orig_seq.TrackNumber
    AND orig.Id          = orig_seq.First_Id
    AND orig.LOCode LIKE 'USHOU%'

-- Destination: last stop per shipment
JOIN (
    SELECT TrackNumber, MAX(Id) AS Last_Id
    FROM sealine_locations
    GROUP BY TrackNumber
) dest_seq ON h.TrackNumber = dest_seq.TrackNumber
JOIN sealine_locations dest
    ON  dest.TrackNumber = dest_seq.TrackNumber
    AND dest.Id          = dest_seq.Last_Id
    AND TRY_CAST(dest.Lat AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL
    AND TRY_CAST(dest.Lng AS FLOAT) IS NOT NULL

WHERE h.Status = 'IN_TRANSIT'

GROUP BY dest.Name, dest.Country, dest.Country_Code, dest.LOCode, dest.Lat, dest.Lng
ORDER BY Shipments DESC
```

**Key logic:**
- `MIN(Id)` per TrackNumber = origin stop → filtered to `USHOU%`
- `MAX(Id)` per TrackNumber = final destination
- Grouped by destination to count distinct shipments per port

python -c "
import pyodbc

conn = pyodbc.connect(
    'DRIVER={ODBC Driver 17 for SQL Server};'
    'SERVER=ushou102-exap1;DATABASE=searates;UID=sean;PWD=4peiling;'
)
cursor = conn.cursor()

# Get all tables
cursor.execute(\"SELECT TABLE_NAME FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE TABLE_TYPE='BASE TABLE' ORDER BY TABLE_NAME\")
tables = [r[0] for r in cursor.fetchall()]
print('Tables:', tables)

# Get all columns for each table
for t in tables:
    print(f'\n=== {t} ===')
    cursor.execute(f\"SELECT COLUMN_NAME, DATA_TYPE, CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH, IS_NULLABLE FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS WHERE TABLE_NAME='{t}' ORDER BY ORDINAL_POSITION\")
    for r in cursor.fetchall():
        print(r)

# Get FK constraints
print('\n=== FOREIGN KEYS ===')
cursor.execute(\"\"\"
SELECT 
    fk.name AS FK_Name,
    tp.name AS Parent_Table,
    cp.name AS Parent_Column,
    tr.name AS Referenced_Table,
    cr.name AS Referenced_Column
FROM sys.foreign_keys fk
JOIN sys.foreign_key_columns fkc ON fk.object_id = fkc.constraint_object_id
JOIN sys.tables tp ON fkc.parent_object_id = tp.object_id
JOIN sys.columns cp ON fkc.parent_object_id = cp.object_id AND fkc.parent_column_id = cp.column_id
JOIN sys.tables tr ON fkc.referenced_object_id = tr.object_id
JOIN sys.columns cr ON fkc.referenced_object_id = cr.object_id AND fkc.referenced_column_id = cr.column_id
\"\"\")
for r in cursor.fetchall():
    print(r)

cursor.close()
conn.close()
"

# sealineDB Schema — searates database

## Connection
See connections.md for credentials.

## Relationships (logical, no FK constraints enforced in DB)

```
Sealine_Header.TrackNumber (PK)
    ├── 1:N → Sealine_Vessels.TrackNumber
    ├── 1:N → Sealine_Locations.TrackNumber     (Id = stop sequence, MIN=origin, MAX=destination)
    ├── 1:N → Sealine_Container.TrackNumber
    ├── 1:N → Sealine_Container_Event.TrackNumber
    ├── 1:N → Sealine_Facilities.TrackNumber
    ├── 1:N → Sealine_Route.TrackNumber
    └── 1:N → Sealine_Tracking_Response.TrackNumber

Searates_Request_Tracking.TrackingNo → Sealine_Header.TrackNumber
ResponseLog.Id → Searates_Request_Tracking.LastResponseId
```

---

## Core Tables

### Sealine_Header
Primary tracking record per shipment.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | PK |
| Type | varchar(10) NOT NULL | e.g. BK (Booking) |
| Sealine_Code | varchar(100) NOT NULL | e.g. CMDU, LMCU, DHC2 |
| Sealine_Name | varchar(500) | e.g. CMA CGM, DHL Global Forwarding |
| API_Status | varchar(500) | success / error |
| Status | varchar(100) | IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED, UNKNOWN, PLANNED, CANCELLED, COMPLETED |
| Is_Status_From_Sealine | int | 1=from carrier, 0=internal |
| Updated_Date | datetime | |
| CreatedOn | datetime | |
| UpdatedDT | datetime | |
| DeletedDt | datetime | soft delete |

### Sealine_Locations
Route stops per shipment. Id is the stop sequence number.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | FK → Sealine_Header |
| Type | varchar(10) NOT NULL | |
| Sealine_Code | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Id | bigint NOT NULL | Stop sequence (1=origin, MAX=destination). Multiple locations can share same Id. |
| Name | varchar(1000) | Location name |
| State | varchar(500) | |
| Country | varchar(100) | |
| Country_Code | varchar(50) | |
| LOCode | varchar(100) | UN/LOCODE e.g. USHOU, SGSIN |
| Lat | varchar(100) | stored as string — use TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) |
| Lng | varchar(100) | stored as string — use TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) |
| Timezone | varchar(100) | |
| CreatedOn / UpdatedDT / DeletedDt | datetime | |

### Sealine_Vessels
Vessels associated with a shipment (1:N).
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | FK → Sealine_Header |
| Type | varchar(10) NOT NULL | |
| Sealine_Code | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Id | bigint NOT NULL | |
| Name | varchar(500) | Vessel name |
| imo | varchar(500) | IMO number |
| call_sign | varchar(500) | |
| mmsi | varchar(500) | |
| flag | varchar(100) | Country flag code |
| CreatedOn / UpdatedDT / DeletedDt | datetime | |

### Sealine_Container
Containers per shipment.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | FK → Sealine_Header |
| Type | varchar(10) NOT NULL | |
| Sealine_Code | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Container_NUMBER | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Iso_Code | varchar(100) | |
| Size_Type | varchar(500) | |
| Status | varchar(100) | |
| Is_Status_From_Sealine | int | |
| CreatedOn / UpdatedDT / DeletedDt | datetime | |

### Sealine_Container_Event
Container-level tracking events.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Sealine_Code / RequestType / Type | varchar | |
| Container_NUMBER | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Order_id | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Location / Facility | varchar(100) | |
| Description | varchar(100) | Event description |
| Event_type / Event_Code | varchar(100) | |
| Status | varchar(100) | |
| Date | datetime | Event date |
| Actual | int | 1=actual, 0=estimated |
| Is_Additional_Event | int | |
| Transport_Type | varchar(100) | |
| Vessel / Voyage | varchar(100) | |
| CreatedOn / UpdatedDT / DeletedDt | datetime | |

### Sealine_Route
Scheduled/actual route dates per location stop.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | FK → Sealine_Header |
| Type / Sealine_Code | varchar | |
| RouteType | varchar(100) NOT NULL | e.g. ETD, ETA, ATD, ATA |
| Location_Id | int | References Sealine_Locations.Id |
| Date | datetime | |
| IsActual | int | 1=actual, 0=planned |
| Predictive_ETA | varchar(100) | |
| CreatedOn / UpdatedDT / DeletedDt | datetime | |

### Sealine_Facilities
Port/terminal facilities per shipment.
| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) NOT NULL | |
| Type / Sealine_Code | varchar | |
| Id | bigint NOT NULL | |
| name | varchar(1000) | |
| Country_Code | varchar(50) | |
| Locode / Bic_Code / Smdg_Code | varchar(100) | |
| Lat / lng | varchar(100) | |

## Request / API Tables

### Searates_Request_Tracking

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|

| LastResponseId | bigint | → ResponseLog.Id |

| isActive | bit | 1=active tracking |

> Searates_Request_Tracking_Deleted — archive of deleted requests (same schema)

### ResponseLog

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| Id | bigint NOT NULL | PK |
| TrackingNo | varchar(100) | |
| RequestType | varchar(10) | |
| Carrier | varchar(100) | |
| URL | varchar(max) | |
| API_Status | varchar(100) | |
| Tracking_Status | varchar(100) | |
| Response | varchar(max) | Raw JSON response |
| StartDate / EndDate | datetime | |
| RetryCnt | int | |
| Batch | varchar(500) | |

### Sealine_Tracking_Response
Batch tracking responses.
| Column | Type |
|--------|------|
| batch | varchar(100) |
| TrackNumber | varchar(100) |
| RequestType | varchar(100) |
| Carrier | varchar(100) |
| Response | varchar(max) |
| Status | varchar(100) |

---

## Reference / Mapping Tables

### API_Configuration
| Column | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| API_NAME | e.g. Sealine_Response |
| URL | Template URL with {key}, {number}, {type}, {sealine} placeholders |
| API_Key | K-23FF78E9-90DA-488C-978A-E98369E87695 |
| MaxRetryCnt | 10 |
| RetryInMin | 20 |
| Daily_API_Limit | 5000 |

### Carrier_Sealine_Mapaping
Maps carrier codes to sealine codes.
| Column | Type |
|--------|------|
| Carrier | varchar(100) |
| Sealine_Code | varchar(100) |
| isDefault | int |

### Response_Sealine_Mapping
Overrides sealine code from API response.
| Column | Type |
|--------|------|
| TrackingNo | varchar(100) |
| NewSealineCode | varchar(100) |

### _EDS_Shipline_code
External carrier code reference.
| Column | Type |
|--------|------|
| shipline_name | varchar(500) |
| carrier_code | varchar(10) |
| createdDT | datetime |

---

## Common Query Patterns

### Get origin (first stop)
```sql
JOIN (SELECT TrackNumber, MIN(Id) AS First_Id FROM Sealine_Locations GROUP BY TrackNumber) o
  ON h.TrackNumber = o.TrackNumber
JOIN Sealine_Locations loc ON loc.TrackNumber = o.TrackNumber AND loc.Id = o.First_Id
```

### Get destination (last stop)
```sql
JOIN (SELECT TrackNumber, MAX(Id) AS Last_Id FROM Sealine_Locations GROUP BY TrackNumber) d
  ON h.TrackNumber = d.TrackNumber
JOIN Sealine_Locations loc ON loc.TrackNumber = d.TrackNumber AND loc.Id = d.Last_Id
```

### Safe lat/lng cast
```sql
TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT), TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT)
```

### Mid-East war zone bounding boxes
```sql
-- Red Sea
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45)
-- Gulf of Aden
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52)
-- Persian Gulf
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60)
-- Eastern Mediterranean
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37)
```

## Common Query Patterns

### Get origin (first stop)
```sql
JOIN (SELECT TrackNumber, MIN(Id) AS First_Id FROM Sealine_Locations GROUP BY TrackNumber) o
  ON h.TrackNumber = o.TrackNumber
JOIN Sealine_Locations loc ON loc.TrackNumber = o.TrackNumber AND loc.Id = o.First_Id
```

### Get destination (last stop)
```sql
JOIN (SELECT TrackNumber, MAX(Id) AS Last_Id FROM Sealine_Locations GROUP BY TrackNumber) d
  ON h.TrackNumber = d.TrackNumber
JOIN Sealine_Locations loc ON loc.TrackNumber = d.TrackNumber AND loc.Id = d.Last_Id
```

### Safe lat/lng cast
```sql
TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT), TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT)
```

### Mid-East war zone bounding boxes
```sql
-- Red Sea
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 12 AND 28 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 32 AND 45)
-- Gulf of Aden
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 10 AND 16 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 42 AND 52)
-- Persian Gulf
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 22 AND 30 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 48 AND 60)
-- Eastern Mediterranean
(TRY_CAST(Lat AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 29 AND 38 AND TRY_CAST(Lng AS FLOAT) BETWEEN 28 AND 37)
```
